admin.oidcGroupsTooltip: Map identity provider groups to registry roles
admin.scimTooltip: SCIM 2.0 user and group provisioning configuration
admin.apiKeysTooltip: Create and manage personal API keys
admin.sourceControlTooltip: Configure SCM providers for module and provider publishing
admin.providerConfigTooltip: Configure provider mirror sources and sync schedules
admin.binariesConfigTooltip: Configure Terraform and OpenTofu binary mirror sources
admin.approvalsTooltip: Review and approve pending mirror sync requests
admin.versionApprovalsTooltip: Review and approve pending mirrored versions
admin.mirrorPoliciesTooltip: Define policies that control what gets mirrored
admin.storageTooltip: Configure backend storage for binaries and providers
admin.securityScanningTooltip: View security scanning configuration and status

about.title: About {{productName}}
about.description: A self-hosted Terraform module and provider registry with support for SCM integration, provider mirroring, Terraform binary distribution, and multi-tenancy.

auth.devModeNotice: Development mode - Click below to login without OAuth
auth.noProviders: No SSO providers configured. Contact your administrator.
auth.ssoInfo: This application uses single sign-on for authentication.
auth.ssoContact: Contact your administrator if you need access.
auth.devLoginFailed: Dev login failed. Check server logs.
auth.ldapLoginFailed: LDAP login failed. Check your credentials.

home.heroSubtitle: Host and manage your custom Terraform modules, providers, and binaries
home.searchAcross: Search across modules and providers in this registry
home.modulesDescription: Browse and use private Terraform modules for your infrastructure.
home.providersDescription: Access custom and mirrored Terraform providers for your cloud resources.
home.terraformBinariesDescription: Download Terraform, OpenTofu, Packer, Sentinel, and OPA binaries from internal mirrors.
home.step1Desc: Authenticate with your organization’s OIDC or Azure AD credentials to access the registry.
home.step2DescAuth: Generate an API key and add it to your Terraform CLI credentials file:
home.step2DescUnauth: Sign in to generate an API key, then add it to your Terraform CLI credentials file:
home.signInToGenerate: Sign in to generate a key
home.step3Desc: Browse modules and providers, then reference them directly in your Terraform configuration using this registry’s hostname.
home.protocolDesc: Full compatibility with Terraform Registry Protocol v1 — use this registry directly in Terraform CLI and provider configurations without any plugins or proxies.
home.setupRequiredDesc: This registry has not been configured yet. Complete the setup wizard to configure authentication, storage, and the initial admin account.
home.featureSetupDesc: New features have been added that require configuration. Complete the setup wizard to enable them.

modules.pageSubtitle: Browse and discover Terraform modules in your organization
modules.loadError: Failed to load modules. Please try again.
modules.noResultsTryDifferent: Try a different search query or sort option
modules.noResultsUploadFirst: Upload your first module to get started

providers.pageSubtitle: Browse and discover Terraform providers in your organization
providers.loadError: Failed to load providers. Please try again.
providers.noResultsTryDifferent: Try a different search query or sort option
providers.noResultsUploadFirst: Upload your first provider to get started

terraformBinaries.pageSubtitle: Download Terraform, OpenTofu, Packer, Sentinel, and OPA binaries from your organisation's internal mirror. Configure your CLI or CI toolchain to point at the mirror URL shown on each detail page.
terraformBinaries.loadError: Failed to load Terraform binary mirrors.
terraformBinaries.noMirrorsBody: Ask an administrator to set up a binary mirror.

detail.noVersionsSynced: No versions have been synced yet.
detail.deprecateDialogBody: Marking this version as deprecated prevents it from being re-synced in future sync runs. It will remain available for download but will be flagged as deprecated.
detail.helpReason: Explain why this version is deprecated, e.g. security vulnerability.

default.title: Help
default.overview: Navigate to a page using the sidebar to see context-sensitive help for that section.

apiReference.heading: API Reference
apiReference.text: Visit the API Docs page for a full interactive reference of all registry endpoints.

home.title: Home
home.overview: The home page provides a quick-start overview of the registry, including live status of available modules, providers, and mirrors. Use it as a jumping-off point to browse published content or access the admin area.

browseModules.heading: Browse Modules
browseModules.text: Click "Modules" in the sidebar to search and explore published Terraform modules.

browseProviders.heading: Browse Providers
browseProviders.text: Click "Providers" to find provider binaries available in this registry.

adminAccess.heading: Admin Access
adminAccess.text: If you have admin privileges, use the sidebar links to manage users, upload content, or configure storage.

modules.title: Modules
modules.overview: Lists all Terraform modules published in this registry. Modules are searchable by name and filterable by namespace. Click any module card to view versions, documentation, and usage instructions.

search.heading: Search
search.text: Use the search field to filter modules by name or namespace in real time.

viewModule.heading: View Module
viewModule.text: Click a module card to open the detail page with version history and README.

uploadModule.heading: Upload a Module
uploadModule.text: Go to Admin → Upload to publish a new module archive.

moduleDetail.title: Module Detail
moduleDetail.overview: Shows all published versions of a specific module, its README documentation, and the Terraform source block needed to reference it. The latest stable version is highlighted.

copySourceBlock.heading: Copy Source Block
copySourceBlock.text: Use the copy button next to the source field to get the Terraform module block.

selectVersion.heading: Select Version
selectVersion.text: Use the version selector to view documentation and inputs/outputs for a specific version.

deprecateDelete.heading: Deprecate / Delete
deprecateDelete.text: Admins can deprecate or remove a version using the action buttons on each version row.

providers.title: Providers
providers.overview: Lists all Terraform providers published or mirrored in this registry. Providers are searchable by type and namespace. Click a provider card to view versions and platform binaries.

search.heading: Search
search.text: Filter providers by name or namespace using the search field.

viewProvider.heading: View Provider
viewProvider.text: Click a card to open the provider detail page showing versions and platform downloads.

uploadProvider.heading: Upload a Provider
uploadProvider.text: Go to Admin → Upload to publish a new provider binary with its checksums.

providerDetail.title: Provider Detail
providerDetail.overview: Shows all published versions of a specific provider and the platform binaries available for each version. Includes the Terraform required_providers block for easy copy-paste.

copyRequiredProviders.heading: Copy Required Providers Block
copyRequiredProviders.text: Use the copy button to get the required_providers configuration.

selectVersion.heading: Select Version
selectVersion.text: Browse platform binaries (OS/arch combinations) per version.

deprecateDelete.heading: Deprecate / Delete
deprecateDelete.text: Admins can deprecate or remove individual provider versions.

apiDocs.title: API Reference
apiDocs.overview: API documentation for all registry endpoints, powered by ReDoc. Browse endpoint groups and view request/response schemas.

authenticate.heading: Authenticate
authenticate.text: Paste your API key as a Bearer token using the Authorize button at the top.

browseEndpoints.heading: Browse Endpoints
browseEndpoints.text: Use the left-hand endpoint tree to navigate by resource group.

dashboard.title: Dashboard
dashboard.overview: A live health snapshot of your registry. The top bar shows mirror health at a glance. The stat cards below break down modules (with per-system counts), providers (manual vs mirrored), Terraform binary mirrors, and total downloads.

healthBar.heading: Health Bar
healthBar.text: Binary Mirrors, Provider Mirrors, and Storage pills turn red when something is failing. Click any pill to jump to that section.

statCards.heading: Stat Cards
statCards.text: Each card is a shortcut — click to navigate. The aside panels on Modules and Providers show the breakdown by system or mirror type.

recentSyncActivity.heading: Recent Sync Activity
recentSyncActivity.text: The table shows the last 8 sync events across binary and provider mirrors, with status, versions synced, and elapsed time.

quickLinks.heading: Quick Links
quickLinks.text: Common admin actions — upload, manage users, configure mirrors — are one click away from the Quick Links panel.

users.title: Users
users.overview: Manage registry user accounts. Users can be created manually for local/API-key auth, or created automatically on first OIDC login. Assign users to organizations and grant them role templates here.

createUser.heading: Create User
createUser.text: Click "Add User" to create a new account with an email and display name.

editUser.heading: Edit User
editUser.text: Click the edit icon on any row to update the user's name, organization, or role.

deleteUser.heading: Delete User
deleteUser.text: Removes the user account. Any API keys they hold will also be invalidated.

organizations.title: Organizations
organizations.overview: Organizations are namespace containers — modules and providers are published under an organization's namespace. Members of an organization inherit its role template's permissions.

createOrganization.heading: Create Organization
createOrganization.text: Click "Add Organization" to define a new namespace with a URL-safe name.

manageMembers.heading: Manage Members
manageMembers.text: Expand an organization row to view and modify its member list.

deleteOrganization.heading: Delete Organization
deleteOrganization.text: Only possible when the organization has no published content.

roles.title: Roles & Permissions
roles.overview: This page shows the fixed role templates available in the registry. Role templates define the permission scopes granted to users within an organization. Templates are system-defined and read-only.

viewScopes.heading: View Scopes
viewScopes.text: Expand a role template to see the full list of permission scopes it grants.

assignToUser.heading: Assign to User
assignToUser.text: Go to Admin → Users to assign a role template to a user's organization membership.

oidcGroups.title: OIDC Groups
oidcGroups.overview: Maps identity provider group claims to registry organizations and roles. When a user logs in via OIDC, their group memberships are read from the ID token and used to automatically provision or update their organization membership. Changes take effect on the next login without requiring a server restart.

groupClaimName.heading: Group Claim Name
groupClaimName.text: Set this to the claim key in your OIDC ID token that carries the user's group list (e.g. "groups"). This must match the protocol mapper configured in your identity provider.

defaultRole.heading: Default Role
defaultRole.text: Optionally assign a fallback role to users whose groups do not match any mapping. Leave blank to grant no automatic membership to unmatched users.

addMapping.heading: Add a Mapping
addMapping.text: Click "Add Mapping" to link an IdP group name to a registry organization and role template. The group name must exactly match the value as it appears in the ID token claim.

roleTemplates.heading: Role Templates
roleTemplates.text: Roles correspond to system role templates (viewer, publisher, mirror_manager, admin). Go to Admin → Roles to review what each template permits.

saveChanges.heading: Save Changes
saveChanges.text: Click "Save Changes" to persist your group claim and mapping configuration. Existing sessions are not affected until the user logs in again.

apiKeys.title: API Keys
apiKeys.overview: API keys allow programmatic access to authenticated registry endpoints. Each key is shown once at creation and stored only as a bcrypt hash — it cannot be retrieved after creation.

createKey.heading: Create Key
createKey.text: Click "Create API Key" to generate a new key. Copy it immediately — it will not be shown again.

revokeKey.heading: Revoke Key
revokeKey.text: Delete a key to immediately invalidate all requests using it.

useKey.heading: Use the Key
useKey.text: Include the key as Authorization: Bearer <key> in HTTP requests.

upload.title: Upload
upload.overview: Upload module archives (.tar.gz) or provider binaries to publish them in the registry. The upload wizard validates archive structure, checksums, and GPG signatures before publishing.

uploadModule.heading: Upload Module
uploadModule.text: Select the "Module" tab, provide namespace/name/system/version, and attach the .tar.gz archive.

uploadProvider.heading: Upload Provider
uploadProvider.text: Select the "Provider" tab, fill in namespace/type/version/platform details, and attach the binary plus checksum files.

scmLinking.heading: SCM Linking
scmLinking.text: Alternatively, link a module to an SCM repository so it publishes automatically from git tags.

scmProviders.title: SCM Providers
scmProviders.overview: SCM provider configurations connect the registry to GitHub, GitLab, Bitbucket, or Azure DevOps for automated module publishing via git tag webhooks. Each connection uses OAuth credentials.

addProvider.heading: Add Provider
addProvider.text: Click "Add SCM Provider", select the platform, enter the OAuth App Client ID and Secret, then complete the OAuth authorization flow.

reconnect.heading: Reconnect
reconnect.text: If OAuth tokens expire, use the reconnect button to re-authorize.

linkModule.heading: Link a Module
linkModule.text: After adding an SCM provider, go to Admin → Upload → SCM to link a repository to a module.

mirrors.title: Provider Mirrors
mirrors.overview: Provider mirrors synchronize provider binaries from the official Terraform registry (or any compatible upstream) into this registry. Once mirrored, providers are available without internet access.

createMirror.heading: Create Mirror
createMirror.text: Click "Add Mirror" to define an upstream registry URL and filter rules for which providers to sync.

triggerSync.heading: Trigger Sync
triggerSync.text: Use the sync button on a mirror to immediately fetch the latest versions from upstream.

viewSyncHistory.heading: View Sync History
viewSyncHistory.text: Expand a mirror row to see the log of recent sync runs and any errors.

storage.title: Storage
storage.overview: Configures the backend storage used for module and provider artifacts. Supported backends are local filesystem, AWS S3, Azure Blob Storage, and Google Cloud Storage. Only one backend can be active at a time.

switchBackend.heading: Switch Backend
switchBackend.text: Select a backend type, fill in credentials, and click "Activate" to migrate to the new storage.

testConnection.heading: Test Connection
testConnection.text: Use the "Test" button to validate credentials before activating.

localStorage.heading: Local Storage
localStorage.text: For development, the local filesystem backend requires no credentials.

terraformBinaries.title: Hosted Binary Mirrors
terraformBinaries.overview: Lists the available binary mirrors hosted by this registry. Each mirror caches a set of binary versions from an upstream source so your CI pipelines and developer machines can download them without direct internet access.

viewVersions.heading: View Versions
viewVersions.text: Click a mirror card to open its detail page, which shows all available versions and the platform binaries (OS/arch) synced for each.

downloadUrl.heading: Download URL
downloadUrl.text: The detail page shows the full download URL pattern. Point your Terraform CLI or CI toolchain at this URL instead of the upstream registry.

adminConfiguration.heading: Admin Configuration
adminConfiguration.text: To add or manage mirror configurations, go to Admin → Terraform Binaries.

terraformBinaryDetail.title: Binary Mirror Detail
terraformBinaryDetail.overview: Shows all versions synced for this binary mirror and the platform binaries available for each version. Expand a version row to inspect individual OS/architecture combinations, checksum verification status, and GPG signature status.

expandPlatforms.heading: Expand Platforms
expandPlatforms.text: Click the expand arrow on a version row to see per-platform details including OS, architecture, filename, and whether checksum and GPG verification passed.

downloadUrl.heading: Download URL
downloadUrl.text: Use the URL pattern shown in the info banner to download a specific version and platform: /terraform/binaries/{name}/versions/{version}/{os}/{arch}.

deprecateVersion.heading: Deprecate a Version
deprecateVersion.text: Admins can click the warning icon to deprecate a version. Deprecated versions remain available for download but will not be re-synced in future sync runs.

deleteVersion.heading: Delete a Version
deleteVersion.text: Admins can permanently remove a version record and its binaries from storage. Consider deprecating instead if you want to retain the files but prevent re-syncing.

restoreDeprecatedVersion.heading: Restore a Deprecated Version
restoreDeprecatedVersion.text: Click the restore icon on a deprecated version to remove the deprecation flag and allow it to be re-synced.

terraformMirror.title: Terraform Binary Mirror
terraformMirror.overview: Manages mirror configurations that cache Terraform and OpenTofu binary distributions from upstream sources. Once mirrored, binaries can be served to engineers without direct internet access, enabling air-gapped or bandwidth-constrained environments.

createMirrorConfig.heading: Create a Mirror Config
createMirrorConfig.text: Click "Add Mirror" to define a new mirror. Give it a name, select the tool type (terraform, opentofu, or custom), and specify the upstream URL to sync from.

syncVersions.heading: Sync Versions
syncVersions.text: Expand a mirror config and click the sync button to immediately fetch the latest binary index from upstream. Sync history shows the result of each run.

inspectPlatforms.heading: Inspect Platforms
inspectPlatforms.text: Drill into a version to see which OS/architecture combinations have been downloaded and are available locally.

enableDisable.heading: Enable / Disable
enableDisable.text: Toggle a mirror config off to pause scheduled syncs without deleting the configuration or cached binaries.

deleteConfig.heading: Delete a Config
deleteConfig.text: Removes the mirror configuration. Previously synced binaries in storage are not automatically removed.

approvals.title: Approval Requests
approvals.overview: Approval requests are raised when a mirror policy requires human sign-off before a provider namespace or binary is mirrored. An admin must approve or reject each request. Approved requests allow the mirror sync to proceed; rejected requests block it.

reviewRequest.heading: Review a Request
reviewRequest.text: Click the "Approve" or "Reject" button on a pending request card to open the review dialog. Add reviewer notes to explain the decision, then confirm.

createRequest.heading: Create a Request
createRequest.text: Click "Create Request" to manually raise an approval for a provider namespace. Useful for pre-approving content before configuring a mirror policy.

filterByStatus.heading: Filter by Status
filterByStatus.text: Pending, approved, and rejected requests are all shown together. Look at the status chip on each card to distinguish them at a glance.

mirrorPolicyLink.heading: Mirror Policy Link
mirrorPolicyLink.text: Each request references the mirror config that triggered it. Navigate to Admin → Provider Mirrors to view or edit the associated mirror configuration.

versionApprovals.title: Version Approvals
versionApprovals.overview: When a provider or Terraform binary mirror has "Require approval" enabled, each newly synced version is held in pending approval and hidden from Terraform clients until an administrator approves it — so "latest" means "latest approved version". This page is the review queue for those gated versions.

whyPending.heading: Why a Version Is Here
whyPending.text: Versions appear here only for mirrors with "Require approval" enabled. A synced version stays hidden from the version listing, network mirror index, and downloads until it is approved. Existing versions and non-gated mirrors are unaffected.

statusTabs.heading: Status Tabs
statusTabs.text: Switch between Pending, Approved, and Rejected to see versions in each state. Pending is the queue that needs action; Rejected versions stay permanently hidden.

typeFilter.heading: Filter by Type
typeFilter.text: Use the Provider / Terraform toggle to narrow the list to mirrored provider versions or Terraform binary versions.

approveReject.heading: Approve or Reject
approveReject.text: Use the Approve or Reject button on a pending row to open the confirmation dialog. Add optional notes to record why; approving a Terraform version also recomputes which version is "latest".

bulkActions.heading: Bulk Approve / Reject
bulkActions.text: Tick the checkboxes (or the header checkbox to select all) and use Approve Selected / Reject Selected to act on many versions at once. The result reports how many succeeded and lists any failures.

auditTrail.heading: Audit Trail
auditTrail.text: Expand a row to see its approval history — auto-approvals, manual decisions, who acted, any notes, and the rule that matched for auto-approvals.

autoApprove.heading: Auto-Approval
autoApprove.text: A mirror's auto-approve rules (patch_only, gpg_verified, semver_constraint, delay_hours) can approve low-risk versions at sync time so only versions that need review land here. delay_hours approves on a later sync once the version has aged; for Terraform binaries the gpg_verified rule does not apply at discovery time.

mirrorPolicies.title: Mirror Policies
mirrorPolicies.overview: Mirror policies control which providers are allowed or denied from being mirrored, and whether mirroring them requires an approval request. Policies are matched by upstream registry URL, namespace pattern, and provider name pattern. Higher-priority policies take precedence when multiple rules match.

createPolicy.heading: Create a Policy
createPolicy.text: Click "Create Policy" and fill in the name, type (allow or deny), and pattern fields. Use glob-style wildcards (e.g. hashicorp/* ) in the namespace and provider fields to match multiple providers at once.

allowVsDeny.heading: Allow vs Deny
allowVsDeny.text: An "allow" policy permits matched providers to be mirrored. A "deny" policy blocks them. Deny policies override allow policies at the same priority level.

requireApproval.heading: Require Approval
requireApproval.text: Enable "Requires Approval" on an allow policy to route matched providers through the approval workflow before syncing. Approved requests appear in Admin → Approval Requests.

priority.heading: Priority
priority.text: Policies are evaluated in ascending priority order (lower number = higher priority). Set priority carefully when allow and deny rules overlap.

evaluatePolicy.heading: Evaluate a Policy
evaluatePolicy.text: Use the "Evaluate" button to test how a specific provider namespace/name would be matched against the current policy set without triggering a real sync.

enableDisable.heading: Enable / Disable
enableDisable.text: Toggle a policy off to suspend it temporarily without deleting it. Disabled policies are skipped during evaluation.

auditLogs.title: Audit Logs
auditLogs.overview: A tamper-evident record of every significant action taken in the registry — uploads, deletes, configuration changes, login events, and mirror syncs. Use it for compliance reviews, incident investigation, or general activity monitoring.

filterByResourceType.heading: Filter by Resource Type
filterByResourceType.text: Use the Resource Type dropdown to narrow results to a specific entity class (e.g. module, provider, user, mirror).

filterByAction.heading: Filter by Action
filterByAction.text: Type an action name (e.g. "create", "delete", "login") in the Action field to show only matching events.

filterByUser.heading: Filter by User
filterByUser.text: Enter a user email to see all actions performed by that account.

dateRange.heading: Date Range
dateRange.text: Set Start and End date pickers to restrict results to a specific time window.

viewDetail.heading: View Detail
viewDetail.text: Click any row to open a detail panel showing the full log entry, including resource ID, actor, and metadata.

export.heading: Export
export.text: Use the Export button to download the current filtered result set as CSV or JSON.

securityScanning.title: Security Scanning
securityScanning.overview: Displays the current configuration and results of the module vulnerability scanner. The scanner analyses uploaded module archives for known security issues using an external scanning tool configured by the server administrator.

viewConfiguration.heading: View Configuration
viewConfiguration.text: The top section shows whether scanning is enabled, the scanner tool and version, severity threshold, timeout, and worker/interval settings.

summaryStatistics.heading: Summary Statistics
summaryStatistics.text: Aggregate counts show total scans, pending, clean, findings, and errors at a glance.

reviewScanResults.heading: Review Scan Results
reviewScanResults.text: The recent scans table lists each module with its scan status and vulnerability counts broken down by severity (critical, high, medium, low).

mtls.title: mTLS Certificates
mtls.overview: Shows the current mutual TLS (mTLS) configuration, including whether client certificate authentication is enabled and which certificate subjects are mapped to authorization scopes. All mTLS settings are read-only in the UI — configuration is managed through the server config file.

viewStatus.heading: View Status
viewStatus.text: The status indicator shows whether mTLS is enabled or disabled, along with the CA certificate file path used to verify client certificates.

certificateMappings.heading: Certificate Mappings
certificateMappings.text: The mappings table lists each certificate subject and the scopes it is authorized to use. Clients presenting a matching certificate receive these scopes automatically.

configurationSource.heading: Configuration Source
configurationSource.text: All mTLS settings are sourced from the server configuration file. To modify mappings, update the config file and restart the backend.

scim.title: SCIM Provisioning
scim.overview: SCIM 2.0 enables automatic user and group provisioning from your identity provider (e.g. Azure AD, Okta). This page shows the SCIM endpoint URLs and authentication requirements needed to configure your IdP.

endpointUrls.heading: Endpoint URLs
endpointUrls.text: Copy the SCIM base URL, Users endpoint, and Groups endpoint into your identity provider's SCIM configuration.

authentication.heading: Authentication
authentication.text: SCIM requests require a Bearer token with the scim:provision scope. Create one on the API Keys page and paste it into your IdP's SCIM token field.

supportedOperations.heading: Supported Operations
supportedOperations.text: Users support full CRUD and soft-delete. Groups are read-only and map to registry organizations. Filtering and pagination are supported.

storage.descLocal: Store files on the local file system. Best for development and small deployments.
storage.descAzure: Use Azure Blob Storage for scalable cloud storage with Azure integration.
storage.descS3: Use Amazon S3 or any S3-compatible storage (MinIO, DigitalOcean Spaces, etc.).
storage.descGcs: Use Google Cloud Storage for GCP-native storage with workload identity support.
storage.helpBasePath: Directory where files will be stored (relative to working directory or absolute path)
storage.labelServeDirectly: Serve files directly (recommended for local development)
storage.helpAccountName: Your Azure Storage account name (visible in Azure Portal → Storage Accounts)
storage.helpAccountKey: Primary or secondary access key (Azure Portal → Storage Account → Access keys). Keep this secret.
storage.helpContainerName: Blob container name. The container must exist before activating this backend.
storage.helpCdnUrl: If using Azure CDN, provide the CDN endpoint URL
storage.helpS3Bucket: S3 bucket name. The bucket must already exist in the specified region.
storage.helpS3Region: AWS region where the bucket is located (e.g., us-east-1, eu-west-1)
storage.helpS3Endpoint: For S3-compatible services (MinIO, DigitalOcean Spaces, etc.)
storage.helpS3Auth: default = AWS credential chain (recommended for EC2/EKS with IAM roles) · static = explicit key/secret (local dev or non-AWS S3-compatible) · oidc = web identity token (GitHub Actions, EKS pod identity) · assume_role = cross-account access
storage.helpAccessKeyId: IAM user access key ID. Use secrets management rather than committing credentials.
storage.helpSecretAccessKey: IAM user secret access key. Use secrets management rather than committing credentials.
storage.helpWebIdentityTokenFile: Path to the web identity token file (e.g., for Kubernetes service account tokens)
storage.helpExternalId: Optional external ID defined in the role's trust policy, used for cross-account access
storage.helpGcsProjectId: Required if creating a new bucket
storage.helpGcsAuth: default = ADC / gcloud auth (recommended for GKE) · service_account = JSON key file · workload_identity = keyless federation (GitHub Actions, GKE Workload Identity)
storage.helpCredentialsFile: Path to service account JSON file
storage.helpCredentialsJson: Paste service account JSON directly (alternative to file path)
storage.helpGcsEndpoint: For GCS emulators or compatible services
storage.subtitleAdd: Add a second storage configuration. Once added, you can migrate data from the active backend to this one.
storage.subtitleConfigure: Configure where the registry will store module and provider files. This is required before you can upload artifacts.
storage.infoConfigured: Storage has been configured. Changing storage backends after initial setup may result in data loss. Create a new configuration and activate it only after migrating existing data.
storage.infoMigrateHintBefore: To migrate data to a different storage backend, click
storage.infoMigrateHintAfter: above to configure a second backend. A migration option will appear once a second configuration exists.
storage.confirmDelete: Are you sure you want to delete this storage configuration?

mirrors.pageSubtitle: Configure upstream registry mirroring for providers
mirrors.emptyState: No mirror configurations found. Add one to start mirroring providers from upstream registries.
mirrors.helpName: A unique name for this mirror configuration
mirrors.helpNamespaceFilter: Comma-separated list of namespaces to mirror (e.g., hashicorp, aws)
mirrors.helpProviderFilter: Comma-separated list of provider names to mirror (e.g., aws, google, azurerm)
mirrors.helpVersionFilter: Filter versions to sync: '3.' (prefix), 'latest:5' (latest N), '>=3.0.0' (semver), or comma-separated list
mirrors.helpPlatformFilter: Select platforms to sync. Leave empty to sync all platforms.
mirrors.helpSyncInterval: How often to check for updates
mirrors.confirmDeleteText: Are you sure you want to delete the mirror "{{name}}"? This action cannot be undone. Mirrored providers will remain in your registry.
mirrors.noProvidersSynced: No providers have been synced yet.

terraformMirror.tooltipDeleteVersion: Delete version and its binaries from storage
terraformMirror.errLoad: Failed to load Terraform mirror configurations
terraformMirror.infoBannerAfter: . This endpoint is unauthenticated — configure your CI or developer machines to point at your registry host.
terraformMirror.emptyState: No Terraform binary mirror configurations exist yet. Create one to start mirroring Terraform or OpenTofu binaries.
terraformMirror.helpName: Unique slug used in API paths (e.g. hashicorp-terraform)
terraformMirror.helpUrlOpentofu: OpenTofu releases server (releases.opentofu.org) or a GitHub repo URL (e.g. https://github.com/opentofu/opentofu).
terraformMirror.helpUrlTerraform: Terraform releases are sourced from releases.hashicorp.com.
terraformMirror.helpUrlCustom: URL of the upstream source. Use a releases server URL or a GitHub repository URL.
terraformMirror.helpVersionFilter: Limit versions to sync: "1.9." (prefix), "latest:5", ">=1.5.0" (semver), "1.5.0,1.6.0" (list). Leave blank for all.
terraformMirror.helpPlatformFilter: Select platforms to sync. Leave empty to sync all platforms.
terraformMirror.deleteConfigTextBefore: Are you sure you want to delete mirror
terraformMirror.deleteConfigTextAfter: ? This will delete all synced version records and cannot be undone. Binaries in storage will NOT be removed automatically.
terraformMirror.noVersionsSynced: No versions have been synced yet.
terraformMirror.deleteVersionTextBefore: Are you sure you want to delete version
terraformMirror.deleteVersionTextAfter: ? This will remove the version record and cannot be undone.

versionApprovals.pageSubtitle: Review and approve mirrored versions before they become visible to Terraform clients
versionApprovals.confirmApproveBody: This version will become visible to Terraform clients.
versionApprovals.confirmRejectBody: This version will remain hidden from Terraform clients.
versionApprovals.requiresApprovalHint: New synced versions must be approved before becoming visible to Terraform clients

apiKeys.pageSubtitle: Manage API keys for Terraform CLI authentication
apiKeys.errLoad: Failed to load API keys. Please try again.
apiKeys.errNoOrg: You must be a member of an organization to create API keys.
apiKeys.errCreate: Failed to create API key. Please try again.
apiKeys.errUpdate: Failed to update API key. Please try again.
apiKeys.errRotate: Failed to rotate API key. Please try again.
apiKeys.errDelete: Failed to delete API key. Please try again.
apiKeys.scopesHelp: Select the permissions for this API key. You can only select scopes within your role's permissions.
apiKeys.noScopesAvailable: No scopes available. Please contact an administrator to assign you a role.
apiKeys.selectAtLeastOne: Please select at least one scope
apiKeys.warnNoOrg: You are not a member of any organization. Contact an administrator to add you to an organization before creating API keys.
apiKeys.emptyDescription: Create an API key to authenticate Terraform and CI/CD clients against this registry.
apiKeys.successCreated: API key created successfully! Make sure to copy it now - you won't be able to see it again.
apiKeys.warnNoRole: You don't have a role template assigned. Contact an administrator to assign a role before creating API keys.
apiKeys.roleInfoAfter: . You can only create API keys with scopes that match your role permissions.
apiKeys.helpName: A descriptive name for this API key
apiKeys.helpExpiration: Leave empty for a key that never expires
apiKeys.successRotated: Key rotated successfully! Copy your new key now - you won't be able to see it again.
apiKeys.rotateOldRevoked: The old key has been revoked immediately.
apiKeys.rotateOldValid: The old key will remain valid until {{date}}.
apiKeys.rotateIntroAfter: " will generate a new key. Choose how to handle the old key:
apiKeys.rotateGrace: Keep old key valid for a grace period
apiKeys.graceHelp: The old key will continue to work during the grace period, giving you time to update integrations.
apiKeys.deleteConfirm: Are you sure you want to delete API key "{{name}}"? This action cannot be undone and will break any existing integrations using this key.
apiKeys.usingApiKeysIntro: To use an API key with Terraform CLI, add it to your Terraform CLI configuration:

mirrorPolicies.pageSubtitle: Define allow/deny rules for provider mirroring
mirrorPolicies.emptyState: No mirror policies found. Create one to control which providers can be mirrored.
mirrorPolicies.helpName: A unique name for this policy
mirrorPolicies.menuAllow: Allow — permit matching providers to be mirrored
mirrorPolicies.menuDeny: Deny — block matching providers from being mirrored
mirrorPolicies.helpUpstreamRegistry: e.g., registry.terraform.io (leave blank to match any)
mirrorPolicies.helpNamespacePattern: Glob pattern, e.g., hashicorp or hash* (leave blank to match any)
mirrorPolicies.helpProviderPattern: Glob pattern, e.g., aws or a* (leave blank to match any)
mirrorPolicies.helpPriority: Higher priority policies are evaluated first
mirrorPolicies.confirmDeleteText: Are you sure you want to delete the policy "{{name}}"? This action cannot be undone.
mirrorPolicies.evaluateIntro: Check whether a specific provider would be allowed or denied by the current policy set.

organizations.errLoad: Failed to load organizations. Please try again.
organizations.errSave: Failed to save organization. Please try again.
organizations.errDelete: Failed to delete organization. Please try again.
organizations.errName: Organization name must be 1–64 characters, start with a lowercase letter or digit, and contain only lowercase letters, digits, hyphens, or underscores.
organizations.helpNameInvalid: Must start with a lowercase letter or digit; only lowercase letters, digits, hyphens, and underscores allowed (max 64 chars)
organizations.helpName: Organization name used in registry URLs (e.g., myorg)
organizations.renameWarnPart1: Renaming this organization will update all module and provider namespaces that currently use
organizations.renameWarnPart3: . User memberships are linked by ID and will not be affected. Ensure any Terraform configurations referencing the old name are updated.
organizations.helpIdpName: Name of the identity provider (e.g., SAML IdP name from config)
organizations.confirmDelete: Are you sure you want to delete organization "{{name}}"? This action cannot be undone.
organizations.membersManageDesc: Manage members and their roles in this organization
organizations.membersViewDesc: View members and their roles in this organization
organizations.noMembers: No members in this organization yet

scmProviders.emptyState: No SCM providers configured. Add one to get started!
scmProviders.helpName: Friendly name to identify this SCM connection (e.g., 'GitHub Production', 'Internal GitLab')
scmProviders.helpTenantId: Your Azure AD / Entra Tenant ID (found in Azure Portal → Azure Active Directory → Overview)
scmProviders.helpClientId: OAuth App Client ID from your SCM provider's application or developer settings
scmProviders.helpClientSecretKeep: Leave blank to keep existing secret
scmProviders.helpWebhookSecret: Used to validate webhook signatures from your SCM system. Can be added later.
scmProviders.confirmDeleteText: Are you sure you want to delete the provider "{{name}}"? This action cannot be undone and will remove all associated OAuth tokens and module links.
scmProviders.patIntro: Enter your Bitbucket Data Center Personal Access Token. You can generate one from your Bitbucket account settings under HTTP access tokens.
scmProviders.helpAuthMode: App credentials are admin-managed and shared by all users — no per-user Connect is required. Per-user OAuth keeps the legacy flow where each user connects their own account.
scmProviders.helpGithubApp: From your GitHub App settings: the numeric App ID, the Installation ID (from the installation URL), and the app's RSA private key.
scmProviders.helpAppPrivateKeyKeep: Leave blank to keep the existing private key

approvals.pageSubtitle: Review and manage mirror provider approval requests
approvals.helpMirrorConfigId: The ID of the mirror configuration to request access for
approvals.helpProviderName: Optional — leave blank to request access to all providers in the namespace

users.errLoad: Failed to load users. Please try again.
users.errSave: Failed to save user. Please try again.
users.errDelete: Failed to delete user. Please try again.
users.errErase: Failed to erase user data. Please try again.
users.emptyDescription: Users appear here after they sign in for the first time. Invite teammates by sharing the registry URL and an OIDC or dev-login provider.
users.helpEmail: User's email address. Used as the login identifier and cannot be changed after creation.
users.helpName: Full display name shown throughout the interface
users.helpAssignOrg: Assign this user to an organization to control which resources they can access.
users.helpRoleTemplate: Defines the permissions granted within the selected organization. Manage templates under Admin → Roles.
users.confirmDelete: Are you sure you want to delete user "{{name}}"? This action cannot be undone.
users.eraseWarnPart2: 's personal data. The user record is tombstoned, API keys and organization memberships are deleted, and all sessions are revoked. Audit log entries are preserved with anonymized identifiers.
users.eraseConfirmPart1: To confirm, type the user's email address (
users.ariaConfirmErasure: Confirm erasure by typing the user email

oidcSettings.pageSubtitle: Configure group claim mapping from your identity provider to registry organizations and roles. Changes take effect on the next login without requiring a server restart.
oidcSettings.errSave: Failed to save group mapping settings
oidcSettings.groupClaimDescBefore: Map IdP group claims to registry organizations and roles. The group claim name must match the claim key in your OIDC ID token (e.g.
oidcSettings.helpGroupClaimName: The ID token claim that contains the user's group list
oidcSettings.emptyMappings: No group mappings configured. Click "Add Mapping" to create one.
oidcSettings.helpIdpGroup: The exact group name as it appears in the ID token claim
oidcSettings.helpOrganization: Registry organization name the user will be added to
oidcSettings.removeTextAfter: ? This will not affect existing memberships — it only stops new logins from automatically setting this membership.
oidcSettings.samlReadOnly: SAML group mappings are configured in the server configuration file and are read-only.
oidcSettings.ldapReadOnly: LDAP group mappings are configured in the server configuration file and are read-only.

providerUpload.pageSubtitle: Add a Terraform provider to your registry
providerUpload.chooseTitle: How would you like to add this provider?
providerUpload.chooseSubtitle: Upload a provider binary directly, or configure a mirror to automatically sync providers from the public Terraform Registry.
providerUpload.manualUploadDesc: Package a provider binary as a .zip file and upload it directly. Best for private or custom providers.
providerUpload.providerMirrorDesc: Configure a mirror to automatically sync one or more providers from the public Terraform Registry on a schedule.
providerUpload.helpVersion: Semantic version in format X.Y.Z (e.g., 1.0.0, 2.1.3). Must match binary version.
providerUpload.helpOsCaption: Target operating system for this provider binary
providerUpload.helpArchCaption: CPU architecture for this provider binary (most common: amd64)

moduleUpload.pageSubtitle: Publish a Terraform module to your registry
moduleUpload.chooseTitle: How would you like to publish this module?
moduleUpload.chooseSubtitle: Upload a packaged archive directly, or connect a git repository for automated publishing via webhooks.
moduleUpload.uploadFromFileDesc: Package your module as a .tar.gz archive and upload it directly. Best for one-off or manual releases.
moduleUpload.linkFromScmDesc: Connect a GitHub, Azure DevOps, GitLab, or Bitbucket repository. New versions publish automatically when tags are pushed.
moduleUpload.scmSubtitle: First, define the module identity. Then you'll choose a repository and configure publishing settings.
moduleUpload.helpModuleName: Descriptive name for what the module does
moduleUpload.helpVersion: Semantic version in format X.Y.Z (e.g., 1.0.0, 2.1.3). Use 0.x.x for pre-release.
moduleUpload.helpDescription: Optional. Brief description of what this module does.

securityScanning.pageSubtitle: Module security scanning configuration and status

scimProvisioning.title: SCIM Provisioning
scimProvisioning.description: SCIM 2.0 endpoints allow external identity providers to automatically provision and de-provision users and groups.
scimProvisioning.helpBaseUrl: Use this as the Tenant URL in your identity provider's SCIM configuration.
scimProvisioning.storeWarning: Store the API key securely. It provides full user and group provisioning access.

roles.loadError: Failed to load roles. Please try again.
roles.title: Roles & Permissions
roles.subtitle: View the available roles and their associated permission scopes. System roles are predefined and cannot be modified.
roles.scopesDefine: Scopes define what actions a role can perform in the registry.
roles.clickRole: Click on a role to see its full scope details.

dashboard.title: Dashboard
dashboard.subtitle: Registry health at a glance
dashboard.mirrorIssueAlert: One or more mirrors have failed their last sync. Check the mirror pages for details.
dashboard.noSyncHistory: No sync history yet. Trigger a sync from the mirror pages.

auditLog.emptyTitle: No audit entries match the current filters
auditLog.emptySubtitle: Audit log entries appear when users make changes. Adjust the filters above or publish a module to see one.

scmWizard.selectProviderRepoError: Please select a provider and repository
scmWizard.selectTagError: Please select a specific tag in Step 2
scmWizard.linkError: Failed to link module to SCM
scmWizard.step0Desc: Choose the SCM provider where your module source code is hosted.
scmWizard.noProviders: No active SCM providers found. Please configure an SCM provider first.
scmWizard.step1Desc: Select the repository that contains your Terraform module code. Optionally select a specific tag if you only want to publish one version.
scmWizard.step2Desc: Choose how versions will be published from this repository.
scmWizard.syncAllDesc: All existing tags matching the pattern will be imported, and new matching tags can trigger automatic publishing via webhooks.
scmWizard.publishSpecificDesc: Publishes only the single tag you selected. Auto-publish is disabled — future versions must be published manually or via a new sync.
scmWizard.repositoryPathHelp: Subdirectory path if module is not at repository root
scmWizard.defaultBranchHelp: The main branch for the repository
scmWizard.tagPatternHelp: Pattern to match version tags (e.g., v*, release/*)
scmWizard.autoPublishLabel: Enable automatic publishing on tag push
scmWizard.autoPublishInfo: When enabled, new versions will be automatically published when matching tags are pushed to the repository. Webhooks will be configured automatically.
scmWizard.changeTagHint: To change the tag, go back to Step 2 and select a different one.
scmWizard.noTagSelected: No tag selected. Go back to Step 2 and select a specific tag from the repository browser.
scmWizard.singleTagInfo: Only this tag will be published. Auto-publish and tag pattern are not applicable for single-tag publishing.

quickApiKey.title: Create API key
quickApiKey.orgRequired: You must be a member of an organization to create an API key.
quickApiKey.createdAnnounce: API key created. Credentials snippet is now visible.
quickApiKey.copiedAnnounce: API key credentials copied to clipboard
quickApiKey.copyFailed: Unable to copy to clipboard. Select the text manually.
quickApiKey.tokenWarning: This is the only time this token will be displayed. Copy it and store it securely.
quickApiKey.confirmCloseText: You haven't copied the token yet. Close anyway?

moduleDocumentation.noData: No inputs, outputs, or provider requirements detected for this module version.

consumedBy.title: Consumed by
consumedBy.subtitle: States in State Manager that reference this module.

versionSelector.allDeprecated: All versions of this module are deprecated.

errorBoundary.title: Something went wrong
errorBoundary.description: An unexpected error occurred. You can try again or reload the page.

apiDocumentation.title: API Swagger Documentation
apiDocumentation.subtitle: Interactive API reference — use the Authorize button to authenticate with an API key

adminUserStep.emailMustMatch: This email must match your OIDC provider identity

settingsPage.title: Settings
settingsPage.description: Control which data you share. Essential cookies required for the site to function cannot be disabled. Changes take effect immediately.
settingsPage.errorReporting: Error reporting — helps us fix bugs faster
settingsPage.performanceMonitoring: Performance monitoring — Web Vitals and page load metrics
settingsPage.analytics: Analytics — usage patterns to improve the product

releasesGpgKeys.title: Release Signing Keys
releasesGpgKeys.description: GPG keys used to verify upstream release signatures. Status is derived against the configured warning threshold.
releasesGpgKeys.loadError: Failed to load GPG key status.
releasesGpgKeys.noData: No release signing key data available.
releasesGpgKeys.unsignedTooltip: This tool's upstream publishes no release signature; binaries are verified by checksum only.

scanFindingsModal.title: Scan Findings
scanFindingsModal.parseError: Could not parse individual findings from scanner output.

storageMigration.mustBeDifferent: Source and target configurations must be different.
storageMigration.startWarning: Starting this migration will copy all artifacts from the source to the target storage. This may take a while depending on the number and size of artifacts.

oidc.title: Identity Provider
oidc.subtitle: Choose how users will authenticate to the registry. Select one method below.
oidc.helpIssuerUrl: The OIDC issuer URL. Must serve a .well-known/openid-configuration document.
oidc.helpScopes: Space-separated OIDC scopes. Must include 'openid'. Defaults: openid email profile
oidc.helpUserFilter: LDAP search filter. %s is replaced with the username.

storage.title: Storage Backend Configuration
storage.subtitle: Configure where Terraform modules and providers will be stored.
storage.helpBasePath: Directory path where files will be stored
storage.labelServeDirectly: Serve files directly (recommended for local development)

scanning.title: Security Scanning
scanning.description: Optionally configure a security scanning tool to automatically scan Terraform modules and providers for vulnerabilities when they are published.
scanning.autoInstallDesc: Download the official release, verify the SHA256 checksum, and install the binary on the server.
scanning.versionHelp: Leave blank to install the latest release.
scanning.binaryPathHelp: Path to the scanning tool binary. Leave empty to use the tool from PATH.
scanning.severityHelp: Minimum severity to report (e.g. LOW, MEDIUM, HIGH, CRITICAL)

branding.title: White-Label Branding
branding.description: Customize the registry's appearance for your organization. All fields are optional — leave any blank to keep the built-in defaults. You can change these settings later from the admin panel.
branding.infoAlert: Branding changes take effect immediately. Logo and hero images must be publicly accessible URLs (HTTPS recommended).
branding.productNameHelp: Displayed in the browser title, sidebar, and login page
branding.primaryColorHelp: Hex color code for buttons, links, and accent elements
branding.logoUrlHelp: Displayed in the sidebar header and on the login page (PNG or SVG, recommended height 40px)
branding.heroUrlHelp: Background image on the login page (wide format, min 1200px wide)
